# Explicit allowlist of third-party GitHub Actions allowed in this repo.
#
# The `workflow-guard` CI job fails any PR that introduces a `uses:` line
# referencing an action NOT on this list. Owner-prefix match — version /
# SHA pin can change without an allowlist update.
#
# Why an allowlist:
#   - A new action is a new supply-chain dependency. Its maintainer can push
#     malicious code at any time; we want to make that decision deliberately,
#     not via a drive-by PR.
#   - Pairs with the "SHA-pin all actions" follow-up: once every entry here
#     has a pinned SHA, this file becomes the canonical list of trusted code.
#
# How to add an action:
#   1. Verify the publisher (GitHub Marketplace "by <verified>" badge or
#      direct vendor org).
#   2. Skim the action's source for `${{ github.event.* }}` interpolation
#      in `run:` blocks, network exfil endpoints, etc.
#   3. Add the owner/repo here (one per line, comments OK with `#`).
#   4. In the same PR, add the `uses:` line to the workflow.
#
# Format: owner OR owner/repo (one per line). Wildcards NOT supported on
# purpose — adding `*` defeats the point of the allowlist.

# --- First-party (GitHub / Anthropic / step-security / OSSF) -----------------
actions/                          # actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, actions/github-script, actions/upload-artifact
github/codeql-action              # CodeQL init/analyze/upload-sarif
step-security/harden-runner       # egress audit
anthropics/claude-code-action     # daily-draft drafter
ossf/scorecard-action             # weekly OpenSSF Scorecard
google/osv-scanner-action         # CVE scan for pip deps

# --- Vetted third-party -----------------------------------------------------
DavidAnson/markdownlint-cli2-action   # markdown lint (used in lint.yml)
crate-ci/typos                        # spell-check
lycheeverse/lychee-action             # external link check
gitleaks/gitleaks-action              # secret scan
